question: Where should I begin if I’m just starting LLM Zoomcamp, and what order should I follow each week?
answer_agent:
If you’re just starting LLM Zoomcamp, begin with **the course intro / first week materials** and then follow the **weekly order as published**: go through the **lesson video first**, then the **notebook/lab**, and finally the **homework** for that week.

If you want, I can also help you map out a simple week-by-week study plan.
answer_orig:
Start with the [LLM Zoomcamp docs](https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/llm-zoomcamp/), the [general Zoomcamp logistics docs](https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/zoomcamp-logistics/), and the [LLM Zoomcamp GitHub repository](https://github.com/DataTalksClub/llm-zoomcamp).

You can start whenever you want. The videos and GitHub materials are available, and the deadlines are listed in the [course management platform](https://courses.datatalks.club/llm-zoomcamp-2026/).

A typical workflow is:

1. Watch the lesson videos.
2. Work through the lesson notebooks/code.
3. Read the homework instructions on GitHub.
4. Submit answers through the course platform before the deadline.

Homework is similar to the lesson flow, but uses a different dataset or slightly different task.

question: How do I find my own entry on the leaderboard if I can’t spot my name?
answer_agent:
I’m sorry, but I couldn’t retrieve the FAQ entry because the search system returned an error.

If you’d like, I can try again with a different search term, or you can tell me the course name and I’ll help you narrow it down.
answer_orig:
When you set up your account, you are automatically assigned a random name, such as “Lucid Elbakyan.” Click on the "Jump to your record on the leaderboard" link to find your entry.

If you want to see what your Display name is, click on the "Edit Course Profile" button.

<{IMAGE:image_1}>

- **First field:** This is your nickname/displayed name. You can change it if you want to be known by your Slack username, GitHub username, or any other nickname of your choice. This is useful if you want to remain anonymous.
- **Second field:** Change this to your official name as in your identification documents—passport, national ID card, driver's license, etc. This is mandatory if you do not want "Lucid Elbakyan" on your certificate. This name will appear on your Certificate!

question: Why does the leaderboard show some random name for me instead of my real one?
answer_agent:
This usually happens when your account profile doesn’t have a display name set, or the leaderboard is showing the name linked to your login/SSO account instead of your profile name.

Common reasons:
- Your profile name hasn’t been saved yet
- You’re signed in with a school or Google account that provides a different name
- The platform is using an anonymized/default name for privacy

If you want, I can help you figure out where to check or change it.

question: Why does the homework content keep changing from one day to the next?
answer_agent:
The homework can change from day to day because the course content is updated regularly to match the pace of the class and to reflect what’s being covered. In some cases, assignments may also be adjusted for clarity, to fix issues, or to respond to student progress.

If you’re seeing unexpected changes, check the course announcements or assignment instructions for notes from the instructor. If you want, I can help you figure out whether a specific homework item was updated or revised.
answer_orig:
If the homework title contains `[DRAFT]`, it means the homework is not ready yet.

The homework is ready only when both are true:

- The homework form is open on the course management platform.
- The homework title does not contain `[DRAFT]`.

Until then, the content can still change. Working on the material or homework in advance is at your own risk, because the final version can be different.
